pr.title: Initial cleanup of the Character Data Pointer tables
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Assembly-only cleanup of asm/data.s that made character data pointer tables more symbolic and searchable. The PR added labels at many character data table boundaries, replaced raw absolute address literals with label references, and converted multiple hex-encoded ASCII string blocks into .asciz strings with alignment. No PR body or review discussion was present in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Touched only asm/data.s, with 753 added and 808 deleted lines across 84 hunks.
Added .global labels for many repeated character data pointer table entries, including the sequence lbl_803BDC68 through lbl_803BF1F8, at apparent 0x50-byte table boundaries following lbl_803BDC18.
In lbl_803BF248, replaced many raw absolute .4byte address literals such as 0x803BDC68, 0x803BDCB8, and 0x803BF1F8 with symbolic label references such as lbl_803BDC68, lbl_803BDCB8, and lbl_803BF1F8.

Converted additional hardcoded pointer values in character-related tables to labels, with examples in the diff including 0x803C82EC to lbl_803C82EC, 0x803C7788 to lbl_803C7788, and several entries under lbl_803C1F40/lbl_803C2360/lbl_803C2468.
Added semantic comments for data regions, including '# Standard Character .dat File Names' and replacing '# TODO: What is this table?' with '# Animation .dat File Name Pointers'.

Converted many 4-byte hex ASCII chunks into explicit .asciz strings followed by .balign 4. Examples include 'PlyMario5K_Share_joint', 'PlyMario5K_Share_matanim_joint', 'PlMrYe.dat', 'PlCa.dat', 'ftDataCaptain', 'PlFx.dat', 'ftDataFox', 'PlLk.dat', 'ftDataLink', and 'PlKb.dat'.

Preserved existing NULL terminators and function pointer/table data around converted string regions; the excerpt shows Fox warning strings and function pointer data remaining present after nearby character filename cleanup.
smart_moves: Introduced labels before replacing raw pointer constants, which reduces risk: each numeric address now has an assembler-visible symbol at the target location.
Used symbolic .4byte label references instead of absolute addresses, making future table movement, symbol searches, and cross-reference analysis easier.
Converted ASCII stored as packed .4byte words into .asciz plus .balign 4, improving readability while preserving expected alignment.
Added narrow semantic comments only where the table role was evident from nearby data names, avoiding a large speculative rename of every anonymous lbl_ symbol.
Kept the cleanup isolated to asm/data.s, which made this a low-scope data readability change rather than mixing pointer-table cleanup with C decompilation work.
decomp_lessons: When cleaning data tables, first mark every target boundary with a label, then replace absolute address words with symbolic .4byte references.
For string tables, prefer .asciz with explicit .balign 4 over raw hex .4byte encodings; it is easier to audit and search while keeping layout stable.
Character resource tables often become understandable through repeated filename patterns: PlXX.dat, PlXXColor.dat, PlyCharacter5K*_Share_joint, and PlyCharacter5K*_Share_matanim_joint.
A TODO on an unknown table can sometimes be retired once adjacent strings reveal the table class, as with the animation .dat file name pointer table in this diff.
Even before full struct recovery, symbolic labels and readable strings create anchors for later C declarations, headers, and table typing.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data symbols kept the project’s address-based lbl_803xxxxx naming convention rather than inventing semantic names without full evidence.
Character data filename strings in the excerpt follow short internal file codes such as PlMrYe.dat, PlMrBk.dat, PlCa.dat, PlFx.dat, PlLk.dat, and PlKb.dat.
Character data symbols embedded as strings follow ftData<Character>, with examples ftDataCaptain, ftDataFox, ftDataLink, and ftDataKirby.

Model/resource strings follow Ply<Character>5K{Costume}_Share_joint and Ply<Character>5K{Costume}_Share_matanim_joint patterns, for example PlyMario5KYe_Share_joint and PlyFox5KGr_Share_matanim_joint.
Demo motion filename strings follow ftDemo<Result|Intro|Ending|ViWait|Vi0102|Vi1101>MotionFile<Character>, with examples visible for Mario, Captain, Fox, and Link.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw pointer words like .4byte 0x803BDC68 with .4byte lbl_803BDC68 after defining the label at that address.
Use '.global lbl_...' immediately before labels that need to be referenced elsewhere in asm/data.s.
When converting packed ASCII .4byte sequences to strings, add '.balign 4' after each .asciz to preserve word alignment of following data.
Keep NULL entries in pointer/string tables explicit; the diff retains .4byte NULL sentinels around converted tables.
Use labels to expose table row starts even if the final semantic type is not yet known.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
searchable_terms: Initial cleanup of the Character Data Pointer tables
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that type or rename the character data pointer tables around lbl_803BF248.
Search for follow-up cleanup of asm/data.s character file name tables into C arrays or structs.
Look for later semantic names replacing lbl_803C1F40, lbl_803C2360, and lbl_803C2468.
Search for references to 'Standard Character .dat File Names' or 'Animation .dat File Name Pointers' in later history.
